expectations.text: Confirms that private sector organisations providing ICT services to Australian government agencies are required to comply with the ISM under their contract
expectations.evidence: When you sign a contract requiring ISM compliance, you are contractually and practically subject to the same obligations as the agency itself for the systems and data in scope.

expectations.text: States that cloud service providers must be assessed using ASD Cloud Security Guidance and the ISM Chapter 3 (Procurement and Outsourcing) requirements apply
expectations.evidence: Chapter 3 (Procurement & Outsourcing) explicitly requires government agencies to ensure their outsourced service providers and cloud vendors implement ISM-aligned security controls. The response also references the ASD Cloud Services Certification Program (CSCP) for cloud-specific assessment requirements.

expectations.text: Identifies key chapters for cloud providers: Ch. 13 (hardening), Ch. 14 (patch/change management), Ch. 15 (logging/monitoring), Ch. 19 (networking/segmentation), Ch. 20 (cryptography)
expectations.evidence: The response explicitly cites: Priority 2 — System Hardening (Chapter 13); Priority 5 — Patch Management (Chapter 14); Priority 4 — System Monitoring and Logging (Chapter 15); Priority 6 — Network Segmentation (Chapter 19); Priority 3 — Cryptography (Chapter 20).

expectations.text: Explains that IRAP assessment may be required by the contracting agency for the cloud service — especially if handling PROTECTED information — even for private sector providers
expectations.evidence: Yes. In practice, an IRAP assessment will almost certainly be required... Mandatory for systems handling PROTECTED and above... The obligation attaches to the system, not the entity operating it.

expectations.text: Mentions the SSP as a document the cloud provider may need to produce or contribute to
expectations.evidence: Produce a System Security Plan (SSP) — Document system boundary, classification, controls implemented, and risk register. The SSP is described as 'the primary document' and 'the centrepiece of the assessment' throughout the response.
